name: Miff Mole
abstract:
Irving Milfred Mole (March 11, 1898 – April 29, 1961), known professionally as Miff Mole, was an American jazz trombonist and band leader. He is generally considered one of the greatest jazz trombonists and credited with creating "the first distinctive and influential solo jazz trombone style."
His major recordings included "Slippin' Aro…

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Dody Goodman in "Dody in Dixieland"

about_quotes.speaker: Bruce Johnstone
about_quotes.evidence: I was listening to a lot of Dixieland as well, some Louis Armstrong, Jack Teagarden, and Miff Mole and his Little Molars.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bruce Johnstone interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: I played there with Pee Wee Irwin’s band and the trombone player at that time was Miff Mole. ... Like I was a sideman for a while at Nick’s playing, as I said with Muggsy Spanier, Pee Wee Irwin, Jimmy McPartland, Miff Mole.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Don Ewell
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing there with Miff Mole and Darnell Howard and Booker T. Washington.

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: I ran into Miff one day at NBC when we were doing some studio work

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Saunders
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions 'Miff Mole' but immediately pivots to discussing Sid Catlett ('they brought Sid Catlett in... we were very, very close'). There is no statement indicating a personal relationship between the interviewee (Red Saunders) and Miff Mole.

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: And then one time they had a band coming to the Ken Club in Boston with Pee Wee Russell and Max Kaminsky and Miff Mole. And they needed a Boston rhythm section... I was playing seven nights and Sunday afternoon for four or six weeks
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001
